ddls:
CREATE TABLE "communications" (
  "communication_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each communication */,
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the category of the message */,
  "priority" TEXT /* Priority level of the message (routine, urgent, asap, stat) */,
  "subject_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the subject of the message */,
  "message_status_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the message processing status */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the message was submitted */,
  "message_content" TEXT /* Text content of the message */,
  "sender_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who sent the message */,
  "receiver_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user or PHA who will receive the message */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the message */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("communication_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_communications_receiver_id FOREIGN KEY ("receiver_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_communications_message_status_id FOREIGN KEY ("message_status_id") REFERENCES message_status ("message_status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_communications_category_id FOREIGN KEY ("category_id") REFERENCES categories ("category_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_communications_sender_id FOREIGN KEY ("sender_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_communications_subject_id FOREIGN KEY ("subject_id") REFERENCES subjects ("subject_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "categories" (
  "category_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each message category */,
  "category_name" TEXT /* Name of the message category */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the message category */,
  "active" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the category is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("category_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "subjects" (
  "subject_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each subject */,
  "subject_name" TEXT /* Name of the subject */,
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the patient involved in the communication */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the subject */,
  "active" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the subject is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("subject_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_subjects_patient_id FOREIGN KEY ("patient_id") REFERENCES patients ("patient_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "patients" (
  "patient_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each patient */,
  "patient_name" TEXT /* Full name of the patient */,
  "date_of_birth" TEXT /* Date of birth of the patient */,
  "contact_info" TEXT /* Contact information for the patient */,
  "gender" TEXT /* Gender of the patient */,
  "address" TEXT /* Address of the patient */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Phone number of the patient */,
  "emergency_contact" TEXT /* Emergency contact information */,
  "active" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the patient is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("patient_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "message_status" (
  "message_status_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each message status */,
  "status_name" TEXT /* Name of the message status */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the message status */,
  "active" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the status is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("message_status_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., clinician, PHA staff, admin) */,
  "password_hash" TEXT /* Hashed password for the user */,
  "last_login" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last login */,
  "active" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the user is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_users_role FOREIGN KEY ("role") REFERENCES roles ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "access_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access event */,
  "communication_id" INTEGER /* ID of the communication being accessed */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user accessing the communication */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date when the communication was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  "user_agent" TEXT /* User agent string from the access request */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("access_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_communication_id FOREIGN KEY ("communication_id") REFERENCES communications ("communication_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "message_history" (
  "history_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each status update */,
  "communication_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the communication */,
  "status_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the message status */,
  "timestamp" TEXT /* Timestamp of when the status was updated */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who updated the status */,
  "notes" TEXT /* Notes or comments about the status update */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("history_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_message_history_status_id FOREIGN KEY ("status_id") REFERENCES message_status ("message_status_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_message_history_communication_id FOREIGN KEY ("communication_id") REFERENCES communications ("communication_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_message_history_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "permissions" TEXT /* Permissions associated with the role */,
  "active" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the role is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "permissions" (
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each permission */,
  "permission_name" TEXT /* Name of the permission */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the permission */,
  "active" INTEGER /* Boolean indicating if the permission is active */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("permission_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "role_permissions" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the role */,
  "permission_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the permission */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id", "permission_id")
)
